channel.title: Artificial intelligence

item.title: OpenAI’s computing deals top $1tn
item.description: Partners including Nvidia, AMD and Oracle have signed up to Sam Altman’s huge bet on the future of artificial intelligence

item.title: How AI became our personal assistant
item.description: New data shows the ways millions have integrated the technology into their everyday lives

item.title: Consultancies must become software groups to survive, IBM executive says
item.description: Industry hopes artificial intelligence implementation will reverse two-year period of sluggish growth

item.title: OpenAI’s circular deals
item.description: OpenAI hopes its deal with AMD will speed the development of data centres to power its models

item.title: Musk names former Morgan Stanley banker as new xAI CFO
item.description: Anthony Armstrong will take over financial management for both the AI group and social media platform X

item.title: Sam Altman has a new project: building AI Inc
item.description: OpenAI is building a corporate empire of mutual dependencies

item.title: Deloitte issues refund for error-ridden Australian government report that used AI
item.description: Big Four firm will repay final instalment after incorrect references and citations found in document

item.title: OpenAI targets 10% AMD stake via multibillion-dollar chip deal
item.description: Latest circular transaction aims to make ChatGPT maker central to the effort to build a huge AI infrastructure

item.title: AstraZeneca signs $555mn AI deal to identify immunology targets
item.description: Pharmaceutical company is latest to invest in artificial intelligence to speed drug development

item.title: EU pushes new AI strategy to reduce tech reliance on US and China
item.description: Brussels to unveil plan targeting digital sovereignty as it warns technology can be ‘weaponised’ by geopolitical rivals

item.title: OpenAI and Jony Ive grapple with technical issues on secretive AI device
item.description: ChatGPT maker is working with former Apple design boss to launch a palm-sized personal assistant next year

item.title: BlackRock’s GIP in talks for data centre deal worth almost $40bn
item.description: Agreement for Macquarie-owned Aligned Data Centers may come as soon as next week as AI boom fuels demand

item.title: Jeff Bezos hails AI boom as ‘good’ kind of bubble
item.description: Amazon founder’s comments follow warning by Goldman Sachs’ David Solomon that ‘it’s not different this time’

item.title: Why is this funny? And why AI doesn’t know — yet
item.description: The longtime New Yorker cartoon ed who turned to writing papers about AI humour

item.title: The AI capex endgame is approaching
item.description: The rapid building of excess capacity both extends bubbles and ultimately bursts them

item.title: Convicted moguls Sean Combs and Miles Guo ponder AI platform after jail
item.description: Chinese businessman tells judge ahead of rapper’s sentencing hearing they ‘sleep right across from each other’ in Brooklyn jail

item.title: Risk of AI bioweapons laid bare by flaw in security software, scientists warn
item.description: Researchers find vulnerability in screening that guards access to dangerous genetic material

item.title: AI comes to the video wars
item.description: A new period of experimentation in apps has arrived to expand the user experience beyond chatbots

item.title: Walmart steps up automation with labour-saving sensors
item.description: Retailer expands use of devices to monitor 90mn pallet deliveries a year from warehouses to its 4,600 US outlets

item.title: Music labels close to landmark AI licensing deals
item.description: Universal and Warner seek payment structure similar to streaming as more disruption looms

item.title: Data centre developer Fermi surges on US market debut
item.description: Investors flock to real estate start-up tied to AI and co-founded by former US energy secretary Rick Perry

item.title: Meta to mine AI interactions to help target advertising
item.description: Changes signal how the social media giant will make money from its costly artificial intelligence efforts

item.title: An AI chart crime compendium
item.description: Looks like everyone’s been doing lines

item.title: GDP - AI = 0?
item.description: Plus, who is paying the tariffs?

item.title: AI and Europe’s quest for a $1tn company
item.description: Swedish start-ups show dilemma over choosing between selling up or betting on becoming huge
